title: Track Selection — Binding Rationale
description:
This is the last thing you write as a foundation intern.

Choose one track — SOC Analysis, Ethical Hacking, or GRC — and write a rationale for your selection that would convince a sceptical senior. You may only pick one, and once graded the selection is final.

Your rationale must address, in order:

  1. The specific work from Stages 0–3 where you felt most alive — name the task, name what clicked.
  2. The specific work you found tedious — and be honest about it; evaluators distrust candidates who claim to have loved everything equally.
  3. A one-year professional goal inside your chosen track — concrete enough that failure would be visible.
  4. One skill gap you will close in the first 90 days of track specialisation, and how.
  5. A sentence naming the other two tracks and why they are not your fit — written without dismissal.

Scoring: self-awareness is rewarded more than polish. Generic rationales that would apply to any candidate earn partial credit only. Evaluators look for evidence you have read your own work from Stages 1–3 and drawn a conclusion from it — not evidence you read a careers blog.

Keep the whole piece under 500 words. The chair reads the last paragraph first.
